message:
feat(charmed): demo showcase improvements and documentation

Jobs Page:
- Improve jobs page rendering in demo showcase
- Add better visual organization

Demo Showcase Library:
- Add lib.rs for demo_showcase crate
- Export public APIs for testing

README:
- Update documentation

Sync beads issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -21,7 +21,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1cgb","title":"EPIC: E2E Test Coverage with Detailed Logging","description":"End-to-end tests for complete application workflows with comprehensive logging.

## Philosophy
- Test full user workflows, not just units
- Detailed logging for debugging test failures
- Capture terminal state at each step
- Support both headless and interactive modes

## Target Applications
1. demo_showcase - primary E2E target
   - All 8 pages (Dashboard, Services, Jobs, Logs, Docs, Files, Wizard, Settings)
   - Navigation flows (sidebar, keyboard shortcuts)
   - Theme switching and persistence
   - Shell-out and restore patterns
   - Wizard deployment workflow

2. glow - markdown viewer
   - File loading and rendering
   - Navigation and search
   - Stash operations

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp every test step
- Log all key presses and expected outcomes
- Capture full terminal state on failure
- Include ANSI sequence dumps for debugging
- Log timing information for performance tests","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:54.027535577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:55.306157878Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cgb","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:54.034668586Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-1cgb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"E2E LOGGING REQUIREMENTS - Non-Negotiable:

Every E2E test MUST include detailed logging that makes debugging failures possible
without re-running the test interactively. This is critical for CI/CD reliability.

REQUIRED LOG CONTENT:
1. Test identification (name, timestamp, environment)
2. Each test step with description
3. All input events (key presses, mouse clicks)
4. Expected outcome for each assertion
5. Actual outcome (pass/fail with details)
6. Terminal state capture on failure
7. Timing information for performance analysis

LOG FORMAT EXAMPLE:
```
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.123Z] TEST_START: test_wizard_deployment_flow
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.124Z] ENV: TERM=xterm-256color, COLORTERM=truecolor
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.125Z] STEP: Initialize application
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.130Z] STEP: Navigate to Wizard page (key: '7')
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.131Z] INPUT: KeyEvent { code: Char('7'), modifiers: NONE }
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.135Z] ASSERT: current_page == Page::Wizard
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.135Z] RESULT: PASS
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.136Z] STEP: Enter service name 'test-service'
...
```

The infrastructure task (bd-28kk) MUST be completed first - all other E2E tests
depend on it for consistent logging.","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dr6","title":"demo_showcase page: Jobs/Tasks (async workflows, progress, filtering)","description":"A realistic CI/job-runner screen: a dense jobs list/table with progress, filtering, and actions that feel like a real ops console.

Why:
- This page is the best “integration crucible” for the stack:
  - bubbletea message routing + command composition
  - bubbles components (table/list, progress, spinner, paginator, timer/stopwatch)
  - harmonica motion for compelling progress/counter animation (optional)
  - charmed_log/toasts for outcomes and errors
- It proves the architecture scales beyond toy demos.

Must showcase (minimum):
- Navigation + selection:
  - keyboard-first (arrows/jk, enter), consistent focus model
  - selection stability across refreshes and filter changes
- Status + progress:
  - determinate progress bars and indeterminate spinners
  - color-coded statuses (running/succeeded/failed/cancelled)
  - durations + ETA-ish hints (timer/stopwatch)
- Realistic operations:
  - start/cancel/retry actions implemented via Cmd/AsyncCmd
  - action results visible (toast + log + UI state)
- Discoverability:
  - query bar + filtering + sorting
  - paginator for large lists
- Details pane:
  - shows job metadata, last logs, parameters, timeline / steps

Data requirements:
- Driven by the deterministic simulation engine and seeded generator.
- Integrates with Wizard: wizard triggers new jobs; jobs correlate to logs.

Acceptance:
- Feels like an actual CI dashboard, not a toy.
- Actions are responsive: input never blocks, state updates are obvious.
- Filtering is fast and predictable.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - filtering/sorting correctness and stability
  - action state machine (start/cancel/retry) transitions
  - progress rendering decisions (spinner vs bar, colors)
- E2E:
  - wizard -> job -> logs correlation scenario
  - smoke tour coverage
  - optional “jobs filtering” scenario if needed for regression.

Notes:
- Keep the information density high, but never sacrifice clarity.
- Make sure `--no-animations` still looks good (no “blank” experience).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:17.395269743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:41:41.424387508Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dr6","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:17.423951863Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fxb","title":"Logs: filtering + search (level/service/query)","description":"Add filtering + search controls to the Logs page.

Why:
- Logs are only useful when you can slice them quickly.
- This is also a great way to showcase bubbles textinput + the app’s focus/keybinding model.

Filters to implement:
- Level selector (e.g., TRACE/DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR)
- Service selector (from demo domain model)
- Free-text query (substring or simple matcher)

Performance requirements:
- Filtering should feel instant.
- Avoid re-rendering/parsing the entire log history on every keystroke.
- Prefer an incremental or pre-indexed filter model that can be unit tested.

UX/polish:
- Highlight matches in the viewport (optional).
- Bracketed paste should work in the query input.
- Show an inline summary: "123 lines (12 matches)".

Acceptance:
- Filtering feels instant and scales to a large buffer.
- Covered by unit tests at the filtering-engine layer.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:38.782877999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:30.352469043Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:30.352332649Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logs","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:38.799160009Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-3jg2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:41.631552397Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:39.044747386Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1fxl","title":"Interactions: focus/blur awareness","description":"Use `FocusMsg`/`BlurMsg` to improve UX and avoid wasting resources when the terminal is unfocused.

Why:
- Focus reporting is an advanced terminal feature that bubbletea supports; the demo should prove it.
- Many TUIs burn CPU animating off-screen. We should behave like a polished app.

Behavior:
- Visual indicator:
  - show a subtle "unfocused" state in the header/status bar (icon + text)
  - reduce contrast of animated/active elements when unfocused
- Optional resource savings:
  - pause or throttle animations
  - pause or slow the background simulation tick

Constraints:
- Must not break determinism in tests:
  - focus/blur should be injectable as messages
  - headless runs should default to focused unless scenario changes it

Acceptance:
- App clearly communicates focus state.
- Unfocused mode avoids needless animation work without breaking the user’s mental model.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.412081335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:33.366234822Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","focus","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.434141459Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:59.432221685Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:00.706143825Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1fxl","title":"Interactions: focus/blur awareness","description":"Use `FocusMsg`/`BlurMsg` to improve UX and avoid wasting resources when the terminal is unfocused.

Why:
- Focus reporting is an advanced terminal feature that bubbletea supports; the demo should prove it.
- Many TUIs burn CPU animating off-screen. We should behave like a polished app.

Behavior:
- Visual indicator:
  - show a subtle "unfocused" state in the header/status bar (icon + text)
  - reduce contrast of animated/active elements when unfocused
- Optional resource savings:
  - pause or throttle animations
  - pause or slow the background simulation tick

Constraints:
- Must not break determinism in tests:
  - focus/blur should be injectable as messages
  - headless runs should default to focused unless scenario changes it

Acceptance:
- App clearly communicates focus state.
- Unfocused mode avoids needless animation work without breaking the user’s mental model.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.412081335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:51:10.380601268Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","focus","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.434141459Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:59.432221685Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:00.706143825Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1g80","title":"E2E scenario: file picker navigate + preview","description":"Add an end-to-end scenario that exercises the Files page (bubbles FilePicker + preview).

Scenario outline:
1) Launch demo_showcase in deterministic headless mode.
2) Navigate to Files.
3) Use the file picker to:
   - move selection
   - enter a directory (if supported)
   - select a file
4) Validate preview behavior:
   - preview pane becomes non-empty
   - breadcrumb / filename reflects the selected entry
   - selecting a markdown fixture renders via glamour (not raw markdown), using the current theme

Notes:
- Avoid relying on the host filesystem. Use the demo’s deterministic fixture directory (from the asset strategy / Config).

Acceptance:
- Deterministic in CI.
- Failure artifacts include the directory listing, selected filename, and the last rendered Files frame.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyLantern","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.191594688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:06.564367389Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:06.564301366Z","close_reason":"Integrated Files page into app (Page enum, Pages struct, routing). Added E2E tests for file picker navigation and preview functionality. Tests verify: fixture mode, j/k navigation, Enter to select/enter, Backspace to go back, and hidden file toggle.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","files","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-1wtl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.230688217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-22c0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:07:58.608108725Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.221867959Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.217633407Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.226320857Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.213212187Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gdn","title":"Add lipgloss individual border edge colors and Transform method","description":"Add per-edge border colors and Transform method to Lipgloss.

Scope:
- Extend border style API to set color per edge (top/right/bottom/left).
- Implement Transform method for style manipulation consistent with Go (e.g., normalize or mutate styles).
- Add unit tests for per-edge colors and Transform behavior.
- Update docs if API changes are user-visible.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Per-edge border colors render correctly in multiple combinations.
- Transform method behaves as documented and has unit coverage.
- Conformance fixtures updated if needed to include per-edge color scenarios.","notes":"Files: crates/lipgloss/src/style.rs, border.rs, renderer.rs. Maintain backward compatibility where practical (but no shims).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:48.923880193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T18:15:01.451539959Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T18:15:01.450257984Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1i7e","title":"demo_showcase: CLI contract (modes, flags, help)","description":"Define the binary UX for launching the demo.

Proposed modes/flags (finalize based on UX concept):
- default: run interactive local TUI
- `--theme <name|auto>`
- `--theme-file <path>` (optional)
- `--seed <u64>` (stable demo data)
- `--no-animations`
- `--no-mouse`
- `--no-color` (force ASCII)
- `--no-alt-screen` (run without alt-screen; useful for debugging + demonstrating bubbletea println/printf)
- `--self-check` (headless CI-friendly render + exit)

Optional subcommands:
- `ssh --addr :2222 --host-key <path>` (wish integration; feature-gated)
- `export --html <path>` / `export --plain <path>` (optional: export snapshots)

Acceptance:
- `--help` is clean and self-explanatory.
- Flags map directly to `demo_showcase::Config` fields.
- Error messages are user-friendly (bad theme name, bad seed, missing file).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.810927082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539935175Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539870655Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:05.884573066Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.830556320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1p61","title":"Unit tests: domain action API correctness","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for the domain action API.

Coverage:
- start job -> job exists + correct initial state
- progress ticks update job correctly
- cancel job -> state transitions + logs/notifications
- retry failed job -> new attempt semantics
- deploy action -> deployment + job correlation

Logging:
- Tests should log action inputs + resulting state diffs for easy debugging.

Acceptance:
- The action API is covered with high confidence and runs fast.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:05.168723242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:24:21.174597921Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:24:21.174533320Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p61","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:05.181377320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1p61","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:19.061233110Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1pdg","title":"Glow: truncate_string handles zero width","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T16:11:52.549379559Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T16:25:02.162307739Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T16:25:02.161950406Z","close_reason":"Completed: handle zero-width truncate_string; add tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1qwj","title":"demo_showcase core: notifications/toasts + status messages","description":"Add transient notifications and persistent status messaging.

Why:
- Real apps communicate state changes (saved, error, connected, job finished).

Implementation:
- Small toast stack or one-line status area.
- Uses lipgloss styles (success/warn/error) and optional harmonica animation later.

Acceptance:
- Pages can emit notifications via app messages; app renders them consistently.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:01.374394082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:10:37.971622922Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:10:37.971559794Z","close_reason":"Implemented notification/toast system with Notification struct, NotificationMsg, and rendering in footer area.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","polish","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qwj","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:01.402524089Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qwj","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:25.545146309Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qwj","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:24.293003527Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1s7t","title":"E2E Tests: Logs page filtering and search","description":"End-to-end tests for the Logs page filtering and search functionality.

## Purpose
Test the log viewer with its filtering, level selection,
and search capabilities.

## Test Requirements

### Log Display
- [ ] Logs render with timestamps
- [ ] Log levels color-coded
- [ ] Long logs truncate/wrap correctly
- [ ] Syntax highlighting in log messages

### Level Filtering
- [ ] Filter by ERROR only
- [ ] Filter by WARN and above
- [ ] Filter by INFO and above
- [ ] Filter by DEBUG and above
- [ ] Filter by TRACE (all)

### Text Search
- [ ] Search filter input works
- [ ] Search highlights matches
- [ ] Search is case-insensitive
- [ ] Clear search shows all

### Navigation
- [ ] Scroll through log list
- [ ] Jump to top/bottom
- [ ] Page up/down
- [ ] Line numbers optional

### Real-time Updates
- [ ] New logs appear at bottom
- [ ] Auto-scroll when at bottom
- [ ] Stay position when scrolled up

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log filter changes
- [ ] Log search queries
- [ ] Log scroll position changes

## Success Criteria
- Filtering works correctly
- Search finds all matches
- Large log volumes perform well","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:21.701891676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:44.463186914Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","logs","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1s7t","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:21.720531569Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1s7t","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:10.316035449Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1s7t","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:44.463158140Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1tdh","title":"Jobs: paginator integration for long lists","description":"Add pagination for long Jobs lists using `bubbles::paginator`.

Why:
- Long lists are a common real-world case; pagination prevents the UI from becoming sluggish.
- It also showcases another Bubbles component in a realistic workflow.

Behavior:
- Paginator reflects total pages and current page.
- Works correctly with filtering/sorting:
  - applying a filter updates total pages
  - selection remains stable (best-effort) when the filtered set changes
- Keyboard:
  - next/prev page shortcuts
  - jump to first/last (optional)

Acceptance:
- Large job sets remain responsive.
- Pagination UX feels consistent with the rest of the app (styled, discoverable).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.084401297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:43.856579389Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","paginator"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tdh","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.103425435Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tdh","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:02.336132860Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1u1s","title":"Unit Tests: bubbletea core (Cmd, Message, Model)","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for bubbletea core functionality.

## Current State
bubbletea has basic tests but gaps in:
- Cmd::batch and Cmd::sequence edge cases
- Message type coercion and downcasting
- Model trait default implementations

## Test Requirements

### Cmd Module
- [ ] Cmd::new() with various closure types
- [ ] Cmd::batch() with empty, single, multiple commands
- [ ] Cmd::sequence() ordering guarantees
- [ ] Cmd::blocking() thread behavior
- [ ] Cmd cancellation (if supported)

### Message Module
- [ ] Message::new() with all message types
- [ ] Message::is::<T>() type checking
- [ ] Message::downcast::<T>() success/failure
- [ ] Message cloning and equality

### Key/Mouse Events
- [ ] KeyEvent parsing from raw bytes
- [ ] MouseEvent coordinate handling
- [ ] Modifier key combinations
- [ ] Special keys (F1-F12, arrows, etc.)

## Success Criteria
- 100% coverage of public Cmd API
- 100% coverage of Message API
- All edge cases documented with tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyLantern","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:21.686576405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:54.673408772Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:54.673341717Z","close_reason":"Added 24 comprehensive unit tests for bubbletea core: Cmd (batch/sequence edge cases, new_optional, blocking, captured values), Message (downcast_ref, Debug format, all built-in types, equality/clone/copy traits). Tests verify 1-command optimization in batch/sequence.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1u1s","depends_on_id":"bd-28co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:21.715496013Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1wtl","title":"Files: preview pane (viewport)","description":"Add a preview pane for the selected file using `bubbles::viewport`.

Why:
- Makes the Files page feel like a real tool (inspect configs/logs/docs quickly).
- Exercises viewport scrolling + resize behavior.

Preview behavior:
- For plain text:
  - show a bounded preview (first N lines/bytes) with a "truncated" indicator
  - choose a clear wrapping strategy (wrap-by-default is fine; optionally support horizontal scroll)
- Show lightweight metadata (optional but nice):
  - file size, last modified, detected type (text/markdown/binary)
- Resize handling:
  - preview reflows correctly on window resize

Acceptance:
- Large files do not lock up the UI; preview work is bounded.
- Scrolling the preview is smooth and does not interfere with file picker navigation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:19.317694097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:21.579944103Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:21.579876477Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","files","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wtl","depends_on_id":"bd-201t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:19.337282014Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1wtl","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:14.307425706Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2xlv","title":"demo_showcase theme: load custom theme from file + contrast checks","description":"Optional but compelling showcase: load a custom theme from disk and validate it.

Why:
- It proves the theming system isn’t hard-coded and can be user-configured.
- Contrast validation demonstrates a mature design approach (readability matters).

Requirements:
- CLI/Config:
  - support `--theme-file <path>` (takes precedence over preset theme)
- File format:
  - pick a single stable format (recommend: TOML or JSON) and document the schema
  - map file values into semantic tokens (not raw ad-hoc styling)
- Validation:
  - validate required fields are present
  - run contrast checks for key pairs (fg/bg, muted/bg, selection/bg)
  - surface warnings in About/Diagnostics and/or as a toast on load
- Failure handling:
  - invalid file should not crash; fall back to a safe preset and show a clear error

Testability:
- Add unit tests for:
  - parsing valid theme files
  - rejecting invalid/missing fields
  - contrast warning behavior

Acceptance:
- Demonstrates lipgloss theme IO + validation in a real workflow.
- The app remains readable and stable even with bad user themes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:27.588370671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:31:46.886871699Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:43.964560626Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:27.601173399Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:40.699647818Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xzk","title":"huh: Form Component Bug Fixes & Robustness","description":"# Epic: huh Form Component Bug Fixes & Robustness

## Background
The huh crate provides interactive form components (Select, MultiSelect, Text, FilePicker).
Deep review identified several bugs and edge cases that affect reliability.

## Issues Identified

### 1. Select Field Missing Empty Guard - FIXED ✓
**Location**: Line 1864 (was missing guard, now fixed)
**Status**: Fixed in this review session

### 2. MultiSelect Cursor Position with Filtering - HIGH
**Location**: Lines 2231, 2185-2198
**Problem**: When filtering is applied, cursor position calculation uses wrong index space.
`self.cursor` indexes unfiltered list but calculation assumes filtered list indices.
**Impact**: Cursor appears at wrong position after filtering.

### 3. Select Field Incomplete Filter Support - MEDIUM
**Location**: Lines 1610, 1815-1875
**Problem**: Select has filter_value field and calls filtered_options(), but navigation
logic doesn't respect filtered state. Navigation may jump to non-visible items.
**Impact**: Poor UX when filtering - navigation broken.

### 4. FilePicker Single-File Edge Case - MEDIUM
**Location**: Lines 3660-3671
**Problem**: With single file, navigation condition prevents selection.
`if self.selected_index < self.files.len().saturating_sub(1)` is always false
when len=1 (0 < 0 is false).
**Impact**: Single-file pickers non-functional.

### 5. Form Initialization Repeated - LOW
**Location**: Lines 4621-4628
**Problem**: init_fields() called on every update while state is Normal and
current_group is 0. Could cause repeated initialization.
**Impact**: Minor performance, potential state corruption.

## Design Philosophy
- All edge cases should be explicitly handled
- Empty/single-item states should work correctly
- Filter state should be fully integrated with navigation
- Defensive programming throughout

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All identified bugs fixed with tests
- [ ] Edge cases for 0, 1, and many items all work
- [ ] Filtering properly integrated with navigation
- [ ] No regression in existing functionality","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:19:12.348259552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:41:09.230039458Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:41:09.229971211Z","close_reason":"All children complete: bd-17p2 (MultiSelect cursor, CLOSED), bd-8wrz (Select filter, CLOSED by WildSnow), bd-1isw (FilePicker single-file, CLOSED by GoldPeak). huh form component bug fixes and robustness hardening is done.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug-fixes","forms","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xzk","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:19:12.365634836Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Acceptance:

 {"id":"bd-2zkx","title":"Integration Tests: Cross-platform terminal support","description":"Integration tests for cross-platform terminal compatibility.

## Purpose
Ensure charmed_rust works correctly across different
terminals and platforms.

## Test Requirements

### Terminal Capability Detection
- [ ] Color support detection (16/256/RGB)
- [ ] Unicode support detection
- [ ] Mouse support detection
- [ ] Alternate screen support

### Graceful Degradation
- [ ] ASCII-only fallback mode
- [ ] 16-color fallback
- [ ] No-mouse fallback
- [ ] Dumb terminal handling

### Platform-Specific
- [ ] Linux terminal emulators
- [ ] macOS Terminal.app
- [ ] Windows Terminal
- [ ] Windows ConHost (legacy)
- [ ] SSH sessions
- [ ] tmux/screen

### Environment Variables
- [ ] TERM handling
- [ ] COLORTERM handling
- [ ] NO_COLOR support
- [ ] FORCE_COLOR support
- [ ] CI detection

## Test Approach
Use environment variable mocking to simulate
different terminal environments.

## Success Criteria
- Works in all major terminals
- Degrades gracefully when features unavailable
- Respects user terminal preferences","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldPeak","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:14.596526172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:23:57.615013775Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:23:57.614885346Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-platform","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zkx","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:14.612515273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-30md","title":"Animations: page transitions + focus highlight","description":"Add high-impact motion polish: page transitions + focus highlight.

Why:
- Smooth transitions are a huge part of the "turned up to 11" feel.
- This also demonstrates harmonica-based animation integrated into bubbletea’s update loop.

What to animate:
- Page transition:
  - subtle slide/fade or wipe between pages (Dashboard → Jobs → Docs, etc.)
  - transition should be fast and never feel sluggish
- Focus highlight:
  - clearly indicate which pane is focused (sidebar vs main vs modal)
  - when focus changes, animate the highlight rather than snapping (unless reduce-motion)

Constraints:
- Must respect `--no-animations` / reduce-motion:
  - animations become instant state changes
- Must never cause layout corruption:
  - transitions should not change measured widths/heights mid-frame

Testability:
- Animation invariants are covered by the animation unit tests.
- Key UI snapshots should include at least one focused vs unfocused view.

Acceptance:
- Transitions feel premium and consistent.
- Reduce-motion mode still looks good (no broken visuals).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:56.791266325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:27:35.043791745Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","polish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30md","depends_on_id":"bd-2fnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:56.825831852Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-30md","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:11.824345470Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-30md","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:10.604501900Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-665u","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Test Coverage Suite","description":"Master epic for achieving comprehensive test coverage across all charmed_rust crates.

## Goals
- Real tests only - NO mocks, fakes, or stubs
- Direct integration with actual terminal/TTY behavior
- Property-based testing for algorithmic/numeric code
- E2E tests with detailed logging for debugging

## Sub-Epics
1. Unit Test Coverage - expand tests in every crate
2. Integration Test Coverage - cross-crate and terminal tests
3. E2E Test Coverage - full application workflows
4. Property Test Coverage - proptest for edge cases

## Success Criteria
- All public APIs have at least one test
- Integration tests verify cross-crate behavior
- E2E tests cover critical user workflows
- Property tests catch edge cases in numeric/algorithmic code","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:17.362248783Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:24.179013006Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-665u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ARCHITECTURE NOTE: This test coverage effort is structured in 4 parallel tracks:

1. **Unit Tests (bd-28co)** - Foundation layer, can start immediately
   - Tests individual functions/methods in isolation
   - NO mocks - use real implementations
   - Priority: Start with bubbletea and lipgloss (most used)

2. **Integration Tests (bd-3nyf)** - Depends on unit tests
   - Tests cross-crate interactions
   - Includes terminal I/O verification
   - Requires PTY test harness

3. **E2E Tests (bd-1cgb)** - Depends on unit + infrastructure
   - Tests complete user workflows
   - Requires logging framework first (bd-28kk)
   - Focus on demo_showcase application

4. **Property Tests (bd-ndn1)** - Parallel with unit tests
   - Uses proptest for random input generation
   - Focus on numeric/algorithmic code
   - Lower priority but high value for edge cases

RECOMMENDED EXECUTION ORDER:
1. Build E2E logging infrastructure (bd-28kk) - unblocks all E2E work
2. Start unit tests for core crates (bubbletea, lipgloss)
3. Add integration tests as unit tests complete
4. Run E2E tests on demo_showcase
5. Add property tests for regression prevention","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6wkl","title":"E2E scenario: mouse scroll + click-to-select","description":"Scenario: validate mouse scroll + click-to-select/focus behavior.

Why:
- Mouse support is a premium feature and easy to regress.
- This scenario proves bubbletea mouse plumbing + demo_showcase hit-testing/mapping is correct in a headless, deterministic way.

Steps (suggested):
- Start the app with mouse enabled.
- Navigate to a scrollable screen (Docs viewport or Files preview).
- Inject mouse wheel events to scroll down, then up.
- Navigate to a clickable/selection surface (Dashboard card grid or Jobs table).
- Inject a mouse click at known coordinates and assert selection/focus changes.
- (Optional) Toggle mouse off in Settings and assert wheel/click no longer changes state.

Assertions:
- Scroll wheel changes viewport offset (rendered output differs and/or a scroll indicator updates).
- Click selects the intended item (visible highlight/focus state changes deterministically).
- No panics; no runaway input loops.

Implementation notes:
- Prefer the E2E runner’s semantic DSL events (`MouseMsg`) for stability.
- If needed, add a “raw byte” mode sub-test that injects an xterm SGR mouse sequence to exercise the InputParser.

Acceptance:
- Catches regressions in mouse mapping and makes failures actionable (step id + last frame + artifact path).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:50:58.031885548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:21.100709336Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","mouse","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:17.148362656Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:21.100671155Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:19.756678314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3ps4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:18.467597486Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:50:58.052269553Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-7jxu","title":"demo_showcase theme: polished UI components (tabs, chips, banners)","description":"Build higher-level styled UI primitives so pages can be assembled like a design system.

Why:
- The demo needs a cohesive, premium visual language.
- Centralizing these patterns prevents ad-hoc styling and keeps pages consistent.

Components to provide (examples):
- Tabs/pills for sub-sections
- Chips/tags for status (ok/warn/error/running)
- Alert banners (info/warn/error) with icons and optional actions
- Empty states (icon + title + guidance)
- Progress-with-label layouts
- Small "stat" widgets (label + value + delta)

Design constraints:
- Must work in:
  - truecolor and 256-color
  - no-color/ASCII mode (fallback styles)
  - narrow terminals (components truncate gracefully)
- Components should be usable from any page with minimal configuration.

Testability:
- Components should be snapshot-testable (render a small representative example set).

- Pages can assemble these like a cohesive design system.

 {"id":"bd-8wlh","title":"glamour: Table Rendering & Markdown Hardening","description":"# Epic: glamour Table Rendering & Markdown Hardening

## Background
The glamour crate renders Markdown to styled terminal output. Deep review identified
issues in table rendering and ANSI handling that affect output quality.

## Issues Identified

### 1. Table Truncation Now ANSI-Aware - FIXED ✓
**Location**: table.rs lines 720-733
**Status**: Fixed in this review session - truncate_content() now properly handles
ANSI escape sequences and adds reset before ellipsis.

### 2. Style Cache Unbounded Growth - MEDIUM
**Location**: syntax.rs lines 568-625
**Problem**: StyleCache uses Vec with linear search and no size limit.
Large code blocks with many styles could consume significant memory.
**Impact**: Memory growth with large documents.

### 3. Language Alias Validation Missing - LOW
**Location**: lib.rs lines 623-630
**Problem**: No validation that target language exists or that aliases don't form cycles.
**Impact**: Silent failures when using invalid aliases.

### 4. Width Calculation Edge Cases - LOW
**Location**: table.rs lines 505-550
**Problem**: If max_table_width is very small (less than overhead), columns collapse to 0.
No validation that min_width * columns <= max_width.
**Impact**: Malformed output with very narrow terminals.

## Design Philosophy
- Graceful degradation for edge cases
- Bounded resource usage
- Defensive validation of configuration
- Clear error messages for invalid input

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Style cache has configurable size limit
- [ ] Language aliases validated on registration
- [ ] Table width calculation handles edge cases
- [ ] All changes have tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:24.212702064Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:07:17.857828709Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:07:17.857763157Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["glamour","markdown","rendering","table"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8wlh","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:24.232680489Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+## Demo Showcase
+
+The flagship demo shows off all functionality in charmed_rust:
+
+```bash
+cargo run -p demo_showcase
+```
+
+### Options
+
+| Flag | Description |
+|------|-------------|
+| `--theme <name>` | Color theme: `dark`, `light`, `dracula`, `nord`, `catppuccin-*` |
+| `--seed <n>` | Seed for reproducible randomness |
+| `--no-alt-screen` | Stay in main terminal buffer |
+| `--no-mouse` | Disable mouse support |
+| `--help` | Show all available options |
+
+### Examples
+
+```bash
+# Run with Nord theme
+cargo run -p demo_showcase -- --theme nord
+
+# Run with Dracula theme
+cargo run -p demo_showcase -- --theme dracula
+
+# Reproducible demo state
+cargo run -p demo_showcase -- --seed 42
+```
+
+### What It Demonstrates
+
+- **Multi-page navigation** - Dashboard, Services, Jobs, Logs, Docs, Files, Wizard, Settings
+- **Theme switching** - Switch themes at runtime with keyboard shortcuts
+- **All bubbles components** - Lists, tables, spinners, progress bars, text inputs, viewports
+- **Markdown rendering** - glamour with syntax highlighting
+- **Form interactions** - Interactive forms via huh
+- **Spring animations** - Smooth physics-based animations via harmonica
+- **File browser** - Navigate the filesystem
+- **Full keyboard & mouse support**
+
+---
+
 ## glow CLI Reference
 
 `glow` is a terminal Markdown reader built on `glamour`:
